related_queries: is there a free AI for writing fiction
free AI story generator no sign up
must_cover_entities: RaptorWrite (Future Fiction Academy — free software, BYO key via OpenRouter)
Inkfluence AI (free export of structured book)
verified_facts: RaptorWrite (RaptorWrite 1.0, Future Fiction Academy): software is free; AI runs through OpenRouter so you pay model providers' rates (BYO key). Base model stays free; premium planned.
Squibler free tier: 6,000 AI words/month + editing for 15 files; also a no-sign-up free story generator.
Toolsaday Novel Studio: advertised free forever.
Inkfluence AI: free export of a structured book (outline + per-chapter + cover/TOC).
Claude free tier: strong fiction prose, generous for conversation.
content_gaps: Nobody clearly separates the THREE kinds of 'free': (a) truly free, (b) free TIER of a paid tool (word caps), (c) free SOFTWARE but you pay AI usage via your own key (BYO). This is the article's core differentiator.
Honest caveat that 'free unlimited' often = BYO-key (you still pay the model).
Which free option is best for actually finishing a draft vs just trying it out.
recommended_angle: Lead with a taxonomy of 'free' (truly free / free tier / free-but-BYO-key), then sort the real tools into those buckets with their real limits, then a 'best free pick for X' section. The taxonomy is the gap every existing result leaves open.
reason: Medium difficulty and a clear differentiation gap. The SERP mixes a vendor post ranking on 'free unlimited' (which is misleading — many are BYO-key) and generic roundups; an honest taxonomy of what 'free' actually means, with real per-tool limits, is genuinely more useful and links naturally to the paid comparison (B2). Good authority-building entry point alongside B2.
